One cat. Two jobs. Everything else is handmade, one small batch at a time.

Room 01 — Inspiration Wall

Everything starts pinned to a wall.

Snack wrappers, convention flyers, a sketch of a cat that wouldn't leave us alone. This is where every collection begins — before it's a product, it's a note on the wall.

Chip Bag Studyreference for the next food-charm run.
Con FlyerStickercon B1 Mall, 25–26 July.
Cat Sketch #14the one that became the mascot.
Color Testmagenta vs. mustard, round two.
Room 02 — The Workbench

Clay, paracord, resin — and a lot of patience.

  1. Sketch the shape until it feels stubborn enough to be a character.
  2. Sculpt in polymer clay, by hand, in small batches.
  3. Cure, sand, and hand-paint every piece.
  4. Seal, attach hardware, and photograph it in the wild.
Room 03 — The Shelf

Small batches. Real scarcity.

Most pieces exist in a handful of colorways and never get remade the same way twice. What's on the shelf now won't be there forever.

Straight off the shelf

Unedited, from the studio floor

The cat has
two jobs.

Si Mozelle is a one-person studio built around a tuxedo cat who supervises everything and helps with nothing. Every piece — sculpted, sanded, and painted by hand — carries a little of that deadpan sense of humor.

est. small batches, big opinions
